zh.lifeSummary: 當Rudolf I von Baden於1227年11月19日出生在德國爾特波格時，他的父親Hermann V. von Baden63歲，母親Irmengard vom Rhein27歲。他於1253年與Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1288年11月19日享年61歲，埋葬在德國巴登-爾特波格巴登-巴登巴登-巴登。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Rudolf I von Baden于1227年11月19日出生在德国爾特波格时，他的父亲Hermann V. von Baden63岁，母亲Irmengard vom Rhein27岁。他于1253年与Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1288年11月19日享年61岁，埋葬在德国巴登-爾特波格巴登-巴登巴登-巴登。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Baden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German and Danish: patronymic from   Bade  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): probably an adoption of the German surname (see 1 above).

English: habitational name from a place called Bayden in Sussex, which is referred to in 17th-century documents but unlocated (it is possibly to be identified with Bainden, a manor in Mayfield, Sussex), or from Baydon in Wiltshire. The latter is from Old English beg + dūn, meaning ‘berry hill’.

variants.name: Bade
meanings: English (North Yorkshire) and Scottish: probably from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Bad(d)a, which is perhaps a short form of various compound names with the first element beadu ‘battle’.
North German: from a short form of an ancient Germanic personal name composed with badu ‘strife, battle’.
North German: occupational name from Middle Low German bade ‘messenger’.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

variants.name: Rudolph
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

en.lifeSummary: When Rudolf I von Baden was born on 19 November 1227, in Württemberg, Germany, his father, Hermann V. von Baden, was 63 and his mother, Irmengard vom Rhein, was 27. He married Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN in 1253. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 19 November 1288, at the age of 61, and was buried in Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rudolf I von Baden est né le 19 novembre 1227, à Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Hermann V. von Baden, avait 63 ans et sa mère, Irmengard vom Rhein, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN en 1253. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 19 novembre 1288, à l’âge de 61 ans, et a été enterré à Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Rudolf I von Baden am 19. November 1227 in Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Hermann V. von Baden 63 und seine Mutter Irmengard vom Rhein 27. Er hat 1253 Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 19. November 1288 im Alter von 61 Jahren und wurde in Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf I von Baden è nato il il 19 novembre 1227 nel Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Hermann V. von Baden, aveva 63 anni e il suo madre, Irmengard vom Rhein, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN nel 1253. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 19 novembre 1288, all'età di 61 anni, e fu sepolto a Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Rudolf I von Badenが1227年11月19日にドイツウuルッテンバーグで生まれた時，父Hermann V. von Badenは63歳，母Irmengard vom Rheinは27歳であった。1253年にKunigunde VON EBERSTEINと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1288年11月19日に61歳で死去し，ドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグバデン-バデンバデン-バデンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Rudolf I von Baden(이)가 1227년 11월 19일 독일 wu르테버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Hermann V. von Baden(은)는 63세였고, 그의 어머니 Irmengard vom Rhein(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1253년 Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1288년 11월 19일 61세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바데-wu르테버그 바데-바든 바데-바든에 묻혔다.

pt.19 November 1227–19 Nov 1288: 19 de novembro de 1227–19 de novembro de 1288
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf I von Baden nasceu em 19 de novembro de 1227, em Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Hermann V. von Baden, tinha 63 anos e sua mãe, Irmengard vom Rhein, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN em 1253. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 19 de novembro de 1288, com 61 anos, e foi sepultado em Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rudolf I von Baden родился 19 ноября 1227 г. в местности вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Hermann V. von Baden было 63 года, а матери, которую звали Irmengard vom Rhein, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN в 1253 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 19 ноября 1288 г. в возрасте 61 год и был похоронен в местности баден-баден, баден-баден, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия.

es.19 November 1227–19 Nov 1288: 19 de noviembre de 1227–19 de noviembre de 1288
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Rudolf I von Baden nació el 19 de noviembre de 1227, en Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Hermann V. von Baden, tenía 63 años y su mamá, Irmengard vom Rhein, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Kunigunde VON EBERSTEIN en 1253. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 19 de noviembre de 1288, a la edad de 61 años, y fue sepultado en Baden-Baden, Baden-Baden, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania.
